pages.raw_text:
After hearing evidence last night,
the city council refused to revoke
Macho's employment Office license.
Evidence in support of graft charg-
es against him was furnished by
Chief of Police Rice, Detectives Nc
Donald and McDermott, License In
spector Gwydir, number of for
eigners who spoke through an in
terpreter, Sheedby and Miller, of
the Peerless employment office;
Jack Travers, and other employ
ment men who claim that Macho's
methods are running the business
in this city.


The city officials testimony dealt
with stories told them by laborers
complaining that they had been
robbed by Macho, but the foreign.
ers' evidence was direct.


Macho had letters and kilograms
from contractors he had sent men
to. stating that no fraud had been
practiced. He also had a couple of
witnesses and testified in his own
behalf. The council decided that
no specific case had been made
against Maoho, in spite of the COM
plants lodged by city officials from
the mayor down, and dismissed the
charges. Attorney Winston de-
Tended the accused. Macho's license
expires In a few weeks.
